title: Request Lines, Methods, Target Forms, Versions, and Routing Inputs · Execution Trace: Target Raw Canonical Boundary
summary: A parser uses Fields on the request line, normalizes targets before validation, treats every target as origin-form, and accepts invalid CONNECT authorities and absolute-form credentials. Planned artifact: Add and verify the target raw canonical boundary increment in strict request-line and request-target parser. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Preserve raw target evidence while parsing URI components, percent encodings, query delimiters, and canonical routing values without double decoding.
Distinguish origin-form, absolute-form, authority-form, and asterisk-form using method and recipient role.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Target Raw Canonical Boundary · Theory Target Raw Canonical Boundary
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 9184 in a municipal gateway receiving one-byte TCP fragments. Build deterministic Go evidence to select the request-target form without normalizing away the raw routing boundary; preserve this operating constraint: one parser state transition may consume bytes only after its full precondition holds; then replace one field with repeated non-combinable lines and preserve their order. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Preserve raw target evidence while parsing URI components, percent encodings, query delimiters, and canonical routing values without double decoding.
